name: Ada Lovelace
title: Full-stack Engineer
tagline: I build typesafe, performant web apps with Deno, TypeScript and Preact.
available: Open to senior IC roles · Q3 2026
about: I'm a full-stack engineer with a decade of experience shipping production software across web, mobile and infra. I care deeply about correctness, instrumentation, and developer ergonomics — in that order.
Most of my recent work has been around end-to-end typed systems built on Deno: APIs, real-time pipelines, and the deploy story that makes them boring to operate.

experience.summary: Building the encrypted password manager and the Howl framework that powers it.
highlights: Designed Howl, a Deno-native full-stack framework — single-pass SSR, file-system routing, file-system APIs with Zod validation
Shipped the Hushkey iOS app and Chrome extension on top of an end-to-end encrypted sync protocol
Cut p99 API latency by 4× by moving from per-instance memory cache to a Redis-backed tiered adapter

experience.summary: Owned the platform team's edge runtime — workers, routing, instrumentation.
highlights: Led the rewrite of the request router from Node to Rust, cutting steady-state CPU by 60%
Authored the team's RFC process; mentored four engineers from mid to senior
Owned the on-call rotation — built the dashboards and runbooks the team still uses

experience.summary: Full-stack feature work across web, billing, and the mobile API.
highlights: Shipped the v2 billing system handling A$30M+ ARR
Built the internal feature-flag service that became a company-wide standard
